_doc: Options for the collect-dex discovery collector. Targets are seeded from the collect-vault cache (underlyingTokens); pools are discovered via Dexscreener token-pairs. counterparty_whitelist lists the USDC/USDT contract addresses (lowercase) per chain that qualify a pool's counterparty side; chain_aliases maps chain ids to CMC DEX network slugs; gecko_aliases maps them to GeckoTerminal network ids. abnormal_price_delta is the price-sanity band used only to pick the per-target history pool (score applies the authoritative $50K + price filters). min_pool_liquidity is the persist floor: a discovered pool is written only when its snapshot liquidity_usd is >= this OR it is the per-target history pool — pools below it feed no score factor. Keep it equal to score's pool_liquidity.min_pool_tvl so the cut stays score-neutral; if you lower score's threshold, lower this and re-collect, else sub-threshold pools score wants would be missing. cmc_batch_size caps how many pool addresses go in one CMC quotes call: the per-chain address list is filtered to plain contract addresses (CMC 400s on pool-id hashes and Curve composite addresses) and chunked into this many per request so a long list does not overflow the URI (HTTP 414); lower it if 414s recur, raise it for fewer round-trips. cmc_networks is the allowlist of networks CMC's DEX API actually serves: chains whose network is absent (e.g. bsc, avalanche — CMC returns 400 'network is not supported') skip the CMC call entirely and use the Dexscreener fallback snapshot. Discovery still covers those chains (Dexscreener), so they stay scored; update this list if CMC's DEX coverage changes.
